title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that converts product signals like errors and user session events into pull request fixes using background agents. The approach embeds LLM-generated queries rather than raw signals for semantic clustering, prioritizes actionable signal types, and uses agent iteration to optimize expensive steps into one-shot calls.
writeup: This talk from PostHog's Joshua Snyder details an automated pipeline that turns product signals like errors and user session events into pull request fixes through background agents. The design choice that matters: it embeds LLM-generated queries rather than raw signals to drive semantic clustering, and it prioritizes signal types that are actionable. Snyder also covers using agent iteration to compress expensive multi-step work into one-shot calls. The takeaway is a concrete architecture for moving from raw product telemetry to code changes with minimal human intervention.
